title: alchemilla ellenbeckii var. hillii
intro: Alchemilla ellenbeckii var. hillii, also known as Hill's lady's mantle, is a plant species in the Rosaceae family, characterized by its white or pale pink flowers and lobed leaves. Traditionally, Hill's lady's mantle has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, making it useful for treating conditions such as hemorrhoids and wounds. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. In herbal preparations, Hill's lady's mantle is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically as a poultice or ointment to aid in wound healing.
